verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family expressed predominantly in testis/sperm. It lacks a transmembrane domain itself but is part of the IZUMO complex on the sperm surface, associating with IZUMO1 (a surface protein) via disulfide-linked interactions. Its surface display is restricted to the sperm lineage, making this a tissue-restricted surface presentation rather than broad somatic accessibility.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether IZUMO4 is stably surface-anchored on sperm via IZUMO1 complex (stable_surface_attachment) vs primarily a soluble/secreted Ig-like protein; exact topology on mature sperm is debated.
